title: Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords
summary: Mauritius signed the Artemis Accords on July 17, becoming the 70th nation to join the international agreement in under six years. This move aligns the island nation with NASA's lunar exploration and sustainable space principles.

title: Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand
summary: The U.S. Space Force increased the maximum value of the National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contract from $5.6 billion to $17 billion to meet rising demand. This move reflects growing needs for national security space launches.

title: Pentagon awards $7.1 million to expand U.S. production of cover glass for satellites
summary: The Pentagon awarded $7.1 million to Martin Materials Solutions under the Defense Production Act to expand U.S. production of radiation-resistant cover glass for satellites. The funding supports spacecraft solar arrays and thermal-control systems.

title: Skyroot Aerospace reaches orbit on first Vikram-1 launch
summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully reached orbit on its first launch on July 18, marking the first commercial Indian rocket to achieve this milestone.

title: NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission
summary: NASA has terminated a lunar lander mission in collaboration with Draper due to significant delays in spacecraft development. The decision marks the end of the planned mission after prolonged setbacks.

title: SpaceX’s only problem is finding more space to work with
summary: SpaceX's IPO has made it a mainstream capital markets story, highlighting the company's vision for interplanetary civilization. However, its growth is now constrained by limited available space for operations.

title: Serbia signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Serbia has joined the U.S.-led Artemis Accords, aligning with the international effort for lunar exploration. This move comes over two years after Serbia signed on to China’s competing lunar base project.

title: SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt
summary: SpaceX aborted the launch of its Starship Flight 13 on July 16 due to engine ignition failures. This marks another setback in the development of the company's massive rocket.

title: SpaceX launches 21 satellites for military data network
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 21 satellites for the Space Development Agency's military data network. The mission brings the agency halfway to completing its Tranche 1 Transport Layer constellation.

title: Nature study identifies a new way to spot nuclear weapons in orbit
summary: A study in Nature Astronomy proposes a new satellite design to detect thermonuclear warheads in orbit. The research is partially funded by unspecified sources.

title: Another reusable rocket? Japan launches, lands RV-X  prototype (video)
summary: Japan successfully conducted the first flight test of its RV-X experimental reusable rocket prototype. The test included both launch and landing, marking a milestone for reusable rocket technology in the country.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 219 — SpaceX Goes to War!
summary: The latest episode of 'This Week In Space' podcast discusses recent space headlines, with a focus on SpaceX's activities. The episode covers major developments in the space industry over the past few weeks.

title: 'We knew we had a lot to live up to': As 'Aliens' turns 40, we chat to the legendary VFX masters who created the Alien Queen (interview)
summary: An interview with VFX masters Shane Mahan and Lindsay MacGowan from Legacy Effects, who worked on the 1986 film 'Aliens' and created the Alien Queen. The article reflects on the legacy and challenges of their work 40 years later.

title: Remembering 'Ulysses 31', the '80s cartoon that transported 'The Odyssey' to outer space
summary: The news reflects on 'Ulysses 31', an 80s cartoon that reimagined 'The Odyssey' in outer space, highlighting its iconic theme song and cultural impact.

title: These 'metallic' dunes on Mars look like sci-fi. What are they really?
summary: New images from ESA's Mars Express orbiter reveal unusual metallic-looking dunes on Mars. Scientists are analyzing these formations to understand their composition and origin.

title: 'Aliens' at 40: Here are 26 reasons why James Cameron's sequel is still the greatest sci-fi action movie of all time
summary: An article celebrates the 40th anniversary of James Cameron's 'Aliens' by listing 26 reasons why it remains the greatest sci-fi action movie. The piece dismisses 'Predator' as inferior to the film.

title: Earth's largest particle accelerator opens new window into the early universe just after the Big Bang: 'A culmination of a decades-long quest'
summary: Earth's largest particle accelerator has opened a new window into the early universe just after the Big Bang, marking a decades-long quest. Researchers believe this observation will provide new insights into the evolution of the early universe.

title: 'The dawn of a new space era': Vikram-1, India's 1st private orbital rocket, aces debut launch
summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully completed its maiden orbital launch, marking India's first private orbital rocket mission and making India the third nation with private orbital launch capability.

title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits
summary: NASA tested a new lightweight, truss-braced wing design (SWEET-15) and found it performed well even beyond intended limits. The 15-foot Structural Wing Experiment Evaluating Truss-bracing showed promising structural capabilities.

title: The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a composite image showing the crescent of Mars growing as the spacecraft approached for a gravity assist in 2026. The images were taken between May 2 and May 15, 2026.

title: Establishing a VTE Risk Score for Astronauts Algorithm
summary: NASA's Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer initiated a working group in April 2026 to review updated VTE case information and discuss research progress on mitigating astronauts' risk of venous thromboembolism during spaceflight.

title: NASA Awards Facilities Support Services Contract for Ames Research Center
summary: NASA has awarded a facilities support contract to Chugach Intelligence Solutions LLC for operations, maintenance, and repair services at Ames Research Center. The five-year hybrid contract ensures the center's facilities remain operational for NASA's missions and partners.

title: NASA’s Chandra and IXPE Study Pulsar in Lighthouse Nebula
summary: NASA's Chandra and IXPE telescopes have released a composite image studying a pulsar within the Lighthouse Nebula. The image combines X-ray data from both observatories to analyze the neutron star's magnetic field and spin.

title: La NASA invita a los medios de comunicación al lanzamiento del telescopio espacial Roman
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the upcoming launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, scheduled for no earlier than August 30 at 7:20 a.m. ET.

title: NASA Invites Media to Roman Space Telescope, Crew-13 Launches
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the upcoming launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope and the SpaceX Crew-13 mission, both scheduled for the coming months. The Roman telescope is set to launch aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from Launch Complex 39A.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured enhanced-color images of Mars' surface during a flyby on May 15, 2026, using its multispectral imager instrument.

title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: The Republic of Mauritius has joined the Artemis Accords as the 70th signatory, becoming the seventh African country to participate in the global coalition for responsible space exploration. NASA's Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson delivered video remarks during the signing ceremony in Ébène.

title: A Tide-Fueled Trove of Biodiversity in Guinea-Bissau
summary: The Bijagós archipelago in Guinea-Bissau hosts diverse ecosystems like mudflats, beaches, and mangroves, supporting migratory shorebirds and sea turtles. This biodiversity highlights the region's ecological significance.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13's Booster 20 aborted during ignition, preventing a planned July 16 launch attempt.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a booster.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: Soyuz MS-29 successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome on July 14. This marks another mission to sustain operations and research aboard the orbital laboratory.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Soyuz is set to launch a crew to the International Space Station (ISS), while SpaceX's Starship is scheduled for its 13th flight test. Seven launches are planned globally this week.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a key milestone ahead of its planned launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: Booster 20, a SpaceX prototype, completed a record-duration static fire test using 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in SpaceX's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company's progress highlights contrasting fortunes for its two launch vehicles.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: New launch pads are being constructed at Cape Canaveral by Stoke Space and Relativity Space as part of the Space Coast's ongoing revitalization. This development highlights the growing commercial spaceflight activity in the region.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets. Three Falcon 9 flights from SpaceX are also expected.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward a planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to serve as a private orbital destination ahead of future missions.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The news provided is incomplete and lacks sufficient details to analyze. No specific space industry news is available for summarization.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 key achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and science.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly propulsion technologies for space missions.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events for industry to discuss the follow-up to the CM25 meeting, presenting key priorities and opportunities in ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The news discusses a new robotic arm designed for versatile applications in the space industry. This innovation could significantly enhance robotic operations in space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and ready to resume formation flying to create artificial eclipses. This marks a significant step forward for the mission's scientific objectives.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports European space science activities by involving research institutes.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical center in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on young researchers to test and validate materials for spacecraft assembly, ensuring they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. These innovative minds contribute fresh ideas to support the success of ESA's missions.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs secured a $10 million seed funding round and relocated its operations to New York City. The company aims to address challenges in spaceflight related to size, weight, and power constraints.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has chosen SpaceX to provide laser communications for the Artemis III mission using Starlink. This contract highlights SpaceX's role in supporting lunar exploration efforts.

title: ISU Founder Shares a Vision Beyond Liquidation
summary: ISU cofounder Bob Richards and Dean Emerita Angie Bukley discussed the aftermath of ISU's liquidation and future plans. The conversation was featured in a Payload article.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: SWISSto12, a European space company, has secured $70 million in Series C funding to expand its operations and meet the rising demand for sovereign space hardware. The investment will support the company's growth in the global space sector.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's proposed mission authorization framework, questioning its financial feasibility. The TraCSS program was a major focus of the discussion.

title: Falcon 9 Sales Freeze Anxiety and Our Launch Ramp Forecast Model for New Rockets
summary: A potential sales freeze on SpaceX's Falcon 9 rockets is causing uncertainty in the space industry, raising concerns about reliable access to orbit for satellite businesses. The situation highlights the impact of SpaceX's launch dominance on the broader space economy.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital acquired new satellite bus inventory to support larger and more power-intensive customer payloads. The purchase aims to expand the company's offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon nominees for space policy leadership faced Senate questions about modernizing military space programs. The nominee for space acquisition highlighted that legacy systems were not designed for current acquisition reforms.

title: Payload Field Guide: NSSL
summary: The U.S. Space Force expanded its National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program by adding two new launch providers on July 8, bringing the total number of companies under the program to seven.

title: Spinifex Space Launches With Australian Test Ranges
summary: Spinifex Space has launched operations to offer suborbital launch campaigns, private range access, and test and evaluation infrastructure. The company will utilize Australian test ranges for these services.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace Partner for Lunar Exploration Ambitions
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to collaborate on lunar exploration. The partnership involves exchanging technical information and conducting system compatibility studies.

title: HawkEye 360 Details Tactical Direct Downlink in Valiant Shield Exercise
summary: HawkEye 360 demonstrated a tactical direct downlink during the Valiant Shield military exercise, showcasing a machine-to-machine pipeline with Lockheed Martin.

title: Starlink Laser Communications Will Support NASA Artemis III
summary: NASA's Artemis III mission will utilize SpaceX's Starlink mini laser terminals for downlinking imagery and video to mission control in Houston. The agreement was announced on Thursday.

title: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness
summary: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness. This is the company's first federal contract.

title: Space Force Chief Nominee Schiess Counts Moving Target Indication Among Top Priorities
summary: U.S. Space Force Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess prioritizes developing space-based moving target indication systems and countering Chinese military space systems as key deterrence goals. His nomination highlights the focus on enhancing U.S. space defense capabilities.

title: Webinar Replay | Space-Based Data Centers: Inside the Race to Move Compute to Orbit
summary: A webinar discussed the emerging industry of space-based data centers, focusing on the business case, technical challenges, and commercial viability of moving computing infrastructure to orbit. Industry players debated the feasibility and future of orbital compute stacks.

title: Keysight and Sateliot to Explore Blockchain Frameworks for 5G NTN Security
summary: Keysight Technologies and Sateliot will explore blockchain frameworks for enhancing 5G non-terrestrial network (NTN) security under an ESA-led program. The initiative aims to detect anomalies in satellite-based 5G communications.

title: Viasat Demonstrates Automotive Voice Call in BMW Platform
summary: Viasat showcased a satellite voice call integrated into BMW's infotainment system during a 5G Automotive Association event. This demonstration highlights advancements in automotive connectivity using satellite technology.

title: AST SpaceMobile Discloses Delays to Launch Campaign, Explores Vertical Integration
summary: AST SpaceMobile has delayed its launch campaign to early 2027 and is exploring vertical integration. The company also initiated a share sale to raise additional funds.

title: Swissto12 Secures $70M in Series C Round
summary: Swissto12 has raised $70 million in a Series C funding round to expand its manufacturing capabilities. The company reports strong revenue growth.
